skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.description: Requires fresh verification evidence before claiming work is complete, fixed, passing, or ready for review.
skill.summary: Run and inspect verification before making completion claims.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives from prose and fenced Markdown examples in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, shell invocations, cryptographic code, reconnaissance behavior, network access, or prompt injection attempts were found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Verification Examples
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged lines 18, 20, 26, 38, and 79-103 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. These locations are fenced Markdown blocks and examples describing verification workflow, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are Markdown fences and checklist examples. There is no script file, interpreter directive, or callable command wrapper in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 3 for weak cryptography, but the line is frontmatter text describing completion verification. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a natural-language description. The apparent crypto match has no semantic relationship to cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Keywords
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md lines 71, 111, and 130 for reconnaissance. These lines discuss verifying agent reports, trust failures, and wording implications; they do not request system or network enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is procedural writing about communication discipline. No commands, targets, host discovery, port scanning, or data collection instructions are present.

content.value_statement: Teams lose trust when agents claim success without proof. This skill makes Claude, Codex, and Claude Code run and inspect fresh verification before reporting completion.
actual_capabilities: Requires fresh command output before any completion or success claim.
Guides agents to identify the command that proves a stated result.
Separates test, lint, build, regression, and requirement verification evidence.
Flags risky language such as should, probably, and looks correct.
Encourages independent review of delegated agent results and VCS diffs.
limitations: Does not run tests automatically by itself.
Does not define project-specific verification commands.
Cannot guarantee correctness when the chosen verification command is incomplete.
Requires the agent to read and report actual command output honestly.

use_cases.title: Pre-PR Verification
use_cases.description: Confirm tests, build output, and review checklist items before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Agent Delegation Review
use_cases.description: Check another agent report against VCS diffs and fresh verification before accepting the result.

use_cases.title: Regression Fix Validation
use_cases.description: Use red-green verification to prove a bug fix addresses the original failure.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Completion Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after finishing a small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before saying this is complete, identify the verification command, run it fresh, read the full output, and report the result with evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Bug Fix Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after changing code for a reported defect.
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify the original bug is fixed. Run the most direct test for the symptom, inspect the output, and state only what the evidence proves.

prompt_templates.title: Pre-Commit Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before committing or preparing a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before committing, verify tests, lint, build, and requirements. Report each command, exit result, and any remaining gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Regression Proof
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a regression test should prove a fix.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform red-green regression verification. Show that the test passes with the fix, fails when the fix is reverted, then passes again after restoration.

output_examples.input: The user asks whether the task is done after a code change.
output_examples.output: The agent identifies the relevant test command, runs it, reads the result, and reports completion only if the output supports it.

output_examples.input: A build appears likely to pass because linting succeeded.
output_examples.output: The agent avoids claiming build success, runs the build command, and reports the actual build status.

output_examples.input: Another agent reports that a delegated task succeeded.
output_examples.output: The agent checks the VCS diff and verifies the changed behavior before accepting or repeating the success claim.

best_practices: Choose the narrowest command that directly proves the claim.
Read the full output and exit status before summarizing results.
Report gaps clearly when verification is partial or fails.
anti_patterns: Claiming success from confidence, intent, or previous command output.
Using lint results as a substitute for build or runtime verification.
Accepting delegated agent results without checking evidence.

faq.question: Does this skill run verification commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to identify and run the right commands before making completion claims.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What counts as enough evidence?
faq.answer: Enough evidence is fresh output from a command or check that directly proves the specific claim.

faq.question: Can this replace project test planning?
faq.answer: No. It improves discipline around verification, but the project still needs correct test and build commands.

faq.question: Why does the skill warn against words like should?
faq.answer: Those words often signal assumptions. The skill requires evidence before positive status statements.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains procedural guidance and Markdown examples, with no executable code or malicious instructions.
